spending.headline: Manage Core Expenses
spending.description: Your essential spending averaged ₹69,000 monthly over the last three months, making up over 72% of your total outgoings. This high proportion indicates limited flexibility for discretionary adjustments.

spending.headline: Review Top Spending
spending.description: Your spending on Grocery, Food, and Shopping totaled ₹102,224 over the last three months. Focusing on these top categories can help you manage your overall spend of ₹329,350.

spending.headline: Leverage Grocery Efficiency
spending.description: Your grocery spending decreased by ₹10,982 from the prior three months to the last three months, creating an opportunity to reallocate these funds.

spending.headline: Trim Frequent Spends
spending.description: Your 87 UPI transactions over the last 3 months, averaging ₹1,448 each, add up to ₹1.26 Lakh. Reducing just 3 such transactions monthly could free up over ₹4,300.

spending.headline: Diversify E-commerce
spending.description: Spending ₹6,800 with one e-commerce platform accounts for 68% of your total online shopping. Exploring other options could offer better value.

spending.headline: Trim Weekend Spending
spending.description: Your weekend spending recently rose to 34% of your total, up from 31%. Reverting to this pattern could free up ₹3,800 monthly.

spending.headline: Review Subscriptions
spending.description: With 6 recurring subscriptions totaling ₹28,400, reviewing your redundant streaming service could optimize your spending.

spending.headline: Boost Liquid Savings
spending.description: Your total bank account balance has decreased by ₹40,000 in the last 90 days. Rebuilding this liquid buffer can enhance your short-term financial resilience.

spending.headline: Streamline Bill Payments
spending.description: Your Airtel Xstream broadband bill for ₹999 was overdue in one of the last four months. Ensuring timely payments can help avoid potential late fees.

spending.headline: Boost Savings Consistency
spending.description: Your savings rate reached 40% with a ₹76,000 surplus in April. Increase your surplus months (3 of last 6) to build reserves faster.

spending.headline: Build Emergency Fund
spending.description: Your emergency fund covers about 2.6 months of average spending. Increasing it by ₹14 Lakh would align it with the ideal 15.6 months of coverage.

spending.headline: Manage Income Swings
spending.description: Your income varied by ₹53,000 over the last five months, while your spending changed by only ₹20,000. This difference means stable spending can be challenging during lower income periods.

spending.headline: Balance Salary Spending
spending.description: 38% of your last month's spending occurred right after salary, up from 35% over 90 days. Spreading this out helps manage funds better.

spending.headline: Maintain Reduced Credit
spending.description: Your credit and pay-later spending was ₹8,650 in the last three months, a ₹700 decrease from the prior period. Continue this positive trend.

spending.headline: Optimize Cash Usage
spending.description: Your average monthly cash withdrawals have decreased by ₹1,934, from ₹6,167 (4-6 months ago) to ₹4,233 (last 3 months). Further reducing cash helps track spending better.
